DIA_TCP.name: Thrombocytopenia
DIA_TCP.description: Thrombocytopenia represents a condition with significantly lower level of blood platelets, that are responsible for stopping bleeding by clumping and forming clots.

DIA_HPL.name: Hemophilia
DIA_HPL.description: Hemophilia is an inherited disease that causes low levels of clotting factors. Without sufficient levels of clotting factors, the blood does not clot properly which leads to excessive bleeding.

DIA_HLA.name: Hemolytic Anemia
DIA_HLA.description: Hemolytic anemia happens due to hemolysis, an abnormal breakdown of red blood cells in the blood vessels or elsewhere in the human body.

DIA_THL.name: Thalassaemia
DIA_THL.description: Thalassaemia is an inherited blood disorder with the formation of abnormal hemoglobin that binds oxygen less efficiently and makes the red blood cell out of shape.

SYM_CRP_HEMOLYSE.name: Haptoglobin - High Level
SYM_CRP_HEMOLYSE.description: Lab tests discovered high level of Haptoglobin.

SYM_CRP_PERITONITE.name: Peritoneal Fluid Infected
SYM_CRP_PERITONITE.description: Peritoneal fluid analysis discovered an infection.

SYM_CRP_ENDOCARDITIS.description: Echocardiography has discovered an infection in endocardium.

SYM_CRP_STK.description: Doppler echocardiography has revealed blockage of brain arteries caused by a clot.

SYM_CRP_TAR.description: Rupture of aorta caused by an injury.

SYM_CRP_THROMBOSIS.description: Ultrasonography has discovered superficial vein trombosis.

SYM_CRP_GALLSTONES.description: Gallstones, also called cholelithiasis, are concretions of cholesterol formed in the gall bladder. Gallstones usually do not cause symptoms, however, if they block the bile duct they can cause a variety of serious conditions.

SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlargement of lymph nodes is recognized as a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, or malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlargement of lymph nodes is recognized as a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, or malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlargement of lymph nodes is recognized as a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, or malignant diseases.

SYM_JM_EAR_FLUID_DRAINAGE.description: Ear discharge is any fluid coming from the ear.

SYM_KN_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlargement of lymph nodes is recognized as a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, or malignant diseases.

SYM_KN_URETHRAL_STONES_DETECTED.name: Urethral Stones Detected
SYM_KN_URETHRAL_STONES_DETECTED.description: Ultrasound examination has discovered some larger stones obstructing the ureter.

SYM_KN_BLADDER_STONES_DETECTED.description: USG has shown stones in bladder.

SYM_JM_SEIZURES.description: Brief episodes caused by an abnormal neuronal activity.

EXM_JM_LARYNGOSCOPY.name: Laryngoscopy
EXM_JM_LARYNGOSCOPY.description: Endoscopial examination of the larynx to obtain view of the vocal cords, glottis. Facilitates tracheal intubation. \n\nRequired room: doctor's office / Observation room / ICU

TRT_CRP_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.description: Intravenous purified blood product containing antibodies that block patient's damaging antibodies. \n\nRequired room: Any office / ward / unit

TRT_OBGYN_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.description: Drugs preventing blood platelets to stick to each other anf forming clots. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at Cardiology dpt

TRT_JM_INSULIN_THERAPY.description: Medication that lowers high sugar levels in blood. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt.

SURG_OTOR_ENDOSCOPIC_CAUTERIZATION.name: Endoscopic Cauterizing
SURG_OTOR_ENDOSCOPIC_CAUTERIZATION.description: Endoscopic cauterizing is surgical procedure through the upper part of digestive tract. Cateurizing is a specialized medical practice of burning tissue to remove or close off a part of it. It is widely used to stop internal bleedings not only during surgeries. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at General Surgery dpt
